name: Future Mod
description: Skip level endings with a key, plus a per-level macro recorder with an automatic frame-perfect analyzer.

skip-end.name: Exit Level Ending
skip-end.description: Press this while a level is finishing to instantly exit the level, skipping the end animation, the delay, and the completion screen. The level still counts as completed. Only active during the ending, so it's safe to set to your jump key (e.g. Space).

macro-enabled.name: Enable Macro Features
macro-enabled.description: Master switch for everything macro-related (recording, playback, the frame-perfect analyzer, the in-level menu, and the HUD). Turn this OFF to fully disable all macro functionality; the 'Exit Level Ending' keybind is unaffected.

macro-record.name: Macro: Record
macro-record.description: Toggle recording your inputs as a step-indexed macro. Because it indexes by physics step (not time), recording while the game is slowed down by other mods still captures clicks correctly. Recording restarts on death, so it captures the attempt you actually complete.

macro-play.name: Macro: Play
macro-play.description: Toggle replaying the recorded macro from the start of the attempt.

macro-analyze.name: Macro: Analyze frame perfects
macro-analyze.description: Run the frame-perfect analyzer on the recorded macro. It shifts each input +/- a few ticks, re-runs, and measures each input's timing window. Turn OFF noclip and speedhack before running.

analyze-speed.name: Analyzer speed-up
analyze-speed.description: Game speed while analyzing. The analyzer first re-verifies your replay at this speed and automatically falls back to 1x if it doesn't stay deterministic. Set to 1 to always analyze in real time.

analyze-releases.name: Analyze releases too
analyze-releases.description: Also measure the timing window of RELEASES (letting go), not just presses. Wave/ship straight-flying is mostly release timing. Roughly doubles the analysis time.

show-240.name: Show 240 FPS
show-240.description: Display the frame-perfect count at 240 (window <= 1 tick).

show-120.name: Show 120 FPS
show-120.description: Display the frame-perfect count at 120 (window <= 2 ticks).

show-60.name: Show 60 FPS
show-60.description: Display the frame-perfect count at 60 (window <= 4 ticks).
